    19вЖТ/-!
    20вЖТ# Erd≈Сs Problem 693
    21вЖТ
    22вЖТ*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/693](https://www.erdosproblems.com/693)
    23вЖТ
    24вЖТ[Er79e] Erd≈Сs, P., _Some unconventional problems in number theory_. Acta Math. Acad. Sci.
    25вЖТHungar. (1979).
    26вЖТ-/
    27вЖТ
    28вЖТopen Real
    29вЖТ
    30вЖТnamespace Erdos693
    31вЖТ
    32вЖТ/-- An integer $m$ has a divisor in the open interval $(n, 2n)$. -/
    33вЖТdef hasDivisorIn (m n : вДХ) : Prop :=
    34вЖТ  вИГ d, d вИ£ m вИІ n < d вИІ d < 2 * n
    35вЖТ
    36вЖТ/--
    37вЖТErd≈Сs Problem 693 [Er79e]:
    38вЖТ

    40вЖТ$A = \{a_1 < a_2 < \cdots\}$ be the set of integers in $[n, n^k]$ which have a divisor
    41вЖТin $(n, 2n)$. Is the maximum gap $\max_i (a_{i+1} - a_i) \leq (\log n)^{O(1)}$?
    42вЖТ-/
    43вЖТ@[category research open, AMS 11]
    44вЖТtheorem erdos_693 :
    45вЖТ    answer(sorry) вЖФ
    46вЖТ    вИА k : вДХ, k вЙ• 2 вЖТ

file.content:
/-
Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# Erd≈Сs Problem 693

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/693](https://www.erdosproblems.com/693)

[Er79e] Erd≈Сs, P., _Some unconventional problems in number theory_. Acta Math. Acad. Sci.
Hungar. (1979).
-/

open Real

namespace Erdos693

/-- An integer $m$ has a divisor in the open interval $(n, 2n)$. -/
def hasDivisorIn (m n : вДХ) : Prop :=
  вИГ d, d вИ£ m вИІ n < d вИІ d < 2 * n

/--
Erd≈Сs Problem 693 [Er79e]:

Let $k \geq 2$ and $n$ be sufficiently large depending on $k$. Let
$A = \{a_1 < a_2 < \cdots\}$ be the set of integers in $[n, n^k]$ which have a divisor
in $(n, 2n)$. Is the maximum gap $\max_i (a_{i+1} - a_i) \leq (\log n)^{O(1)}$?
-/
@[category research open, AMS 11]
theorem erdos_693 :
    answer(sorry) вЖФ
    вИА k : вДХ, k вЙ• 2 вЖТ
    вИГ C : вДХ,
    вИГ NвВА : вДХ, вИА n : вДХ, n вЙ• NвВА вЖТ
    вИА a b : вДХ, n вЙ§ a вЖТ a < b вЖТ b вЙ§ n ^ k вЖТ
    hasDivisorIn a n вЖТ hasDivisorIn b n вЖТ
    (вИА m : вДХ, a < m вЖТ m < b вЖТ ¬ђhasDivisorIn m n) вЖТ
    (b : вДЭ) - (a : вДЭ) вЙ§ (Real.log (n : вДЭ)) ^ C := by
  sorry

end Erdos693

    17вЖТ- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link: `*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/693](https://www.erdosproblems.com/693)`.
    18вЖТ- [ ] **Missing brief plain-English description of the problem in the module docstring.** The problem description (integers with a divisor in $(n, 2n)$, bounding consecutive gaps) appears only in the theorem docstring (lines 37вАУ42), not in the module docstring itself. The module docstring contains only the heading, reference link, and bibliography entry `[Er79e]`.
    19вЖТ- [x] Bibliography entry present: `[Er79e]` with full citation (Erd≈Сs, _Some unconventional problems in number theory_, 1979).

    28вЖТ

    73вЖТ- [x] No `native_decide`.
    74вЖТ

    83вЖТ- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).

    85вЖТ- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).

    87вЖТ

file.content:
# Review: `deepmind/693.lean` вАФ Erd≈Сs Problem 693

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports` at line 17.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line (lines 19вАУ26).
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erd≈Сs Problem 693`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link: `*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/693](https://www.erdosproblems.com/693)`.
- [ ] **Missing brief plain-English description of the problem in the module docstring.** The problem description (integers with a divisor in $(n, 2n)$, bounding consecutive gaps) appears only in the theorem docstring (lines 37вАУ42), not in the module docstring itself. The module docstring contains only the heading, reference link, and bibliography entry `[Er79e]`.
- [x] Bibliography entry present: `[Er79e]` with full citation (Erd≈Сs, _Some unconventional problems in number theory_, 1979).

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos693 ... end Erdos693`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos693`.
- [x] `open` statement appears **before** the namespace block: `open Real` on line 28.

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `hasDivisorIn` has a LaTeX docstring: `/-- An integer $m$ has a divisor in the open interval $(n, 2n)$. -/`.
- [x] No classical choice or nonconstructive operations вАФ returns `Prop`; no `noncomputable` needed.
- [x] No `sorry` in the definition.
- [ ] **Naming violation:** `hasDivisorIn` returns `Prop`, so it is a predicate and should use `UpperCamelCase` per checklist ¬І5: "Types and Props use UpperCamelCase (e.g., `IsCoveringSystem`)." The correct name would be `HasDivisorIn`.
- [x] `local notation` not used вАФ not needed here. N/A.
- [ ] No `@[category test]`/`@[category API]` sanity checks for the definition. *(Optional вАФ checklist says "consider adding".)*

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_693` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib `snake_case` conventions.
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same** attribute line: `@[category research open, AMS 11]`.
- [x] Has a docstring (`/-- Erd≈Сs Problem 693 [Er79e]: ... -/`).
- [x] Docstring references the source paper: `[Er79e]`.

### `answer()` usage

The problem asks: "Is the maximum gap $\max_i (a_{i+1} - a_i) \leq (\log n)^{O(1)}$?" вАФ this is a **yes/no question** (Case 1).

- [x] The theorem type is `answer(sorry) вЖФ P`.
- [x] `answer(sorry)` is used (problem is open/unsolved).
- [x] The RHS formalizes the question as originally asked: `answer(True)` would mean "yes, the maximum gap is bounded by a power of log n."
- [x] Quantifiers (`вИА k`, `вИГ C`, `вИГ NвВА`, `вИА n`, `вИА a b`) appear **after** `answer(sorry)`, not as theorem parameters.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute: `research open`.
- [x] Value `research open` is valid.
- [x] The `research open` theorem contains `sorry` in its proof body.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute: `AMS 11`.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed: `11`.
- [x] Numbers in ascending order (single entry вАФ trivially satisfied).
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] `11` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code (Number theory).

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Uses multi-line `by` / `sorry` pattern (`:= by` on line 52, `sorry` indented on line 53). Acceptable per checklist.
- [x] No `sorry` in any `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure`.
- [x] No informal proof sketches for the open research problem.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- N/A. No variants present; only the main theorem.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `вИА`, `вИГ`, `вЙ•`, `вЙ§`, `¬ђ`, `вЖФ`, `вДХ`, `вДЭ`, `вИ£`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` that disables linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` вАФ not verified in this review.
- [ ] All five custom linters pass вАФ not verified in this review.

---

## Summary

**CONDITIONAL PASS вАФ two issues found:**

1. **Predicate naming (¬І5):** `hasDivisorIn` should be `HasDivisorIn` to follow the UpperCamelCase convention for predicates returning `Prop` (cf. `IsCoveringSystem` example in the checklist).

2. **Module docstring missing problem description (¬І3):** The module docstring contains only the heading, reference link, and bibliography entry. A brief plain-English summary of the problem should be added to the module docstring (currently the description lives only in the theorem docstring).

All other checklist items pass. The formalization correctly identifies the yes/no nature of the problem (Case 1) and places all quantifiers on the RHS of the `вЖФ`. The `answer()` usage, category, AMS attributes, and proof body are all correct.

/-!
# Erd≈Сs Problem 693

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/693](https://www.erdosproblems.com/693)

[Er79e] Erd≈Сs, P., _Some unconventional problems in number theory_. Acta Math. Acad. Sci.

input.new_string:
/-!
# Erd≈Сs Problem 693

Let $k \geq 2$ and $n$ be sufficiently large. Consider the set of integers in $[n, n^k]$ which
have a divisor in the open interval $(n, 2n)$. Is the maximum gap between consecutive elements of
this set at most $(\log n)^{O(1)}$?

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/693](https://www.erdosproblems.com/693)

[Er79e] Erd≈Сs, P., _Some unconventional problems in number theory_. Acta Math. Acad. Sci.
Hungar. (1979).
-/

content.content: The file /home/ryan/erdos/deepmind/693.lean has been updated successfully.

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# Erd≈Сs Problem 693

Let $k \geq 2$ and $n$ be sufficiently large. Consider the set of integers in $[n, n^k]$ which
have a divisor in the open interval $(n, 2n)$. Is the maximum gap between consecutive elements of
this set at most $(\log n)^{O(1)}$?

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/693](https://www.erdosproblems.com/693)

[Er79e] Erd≈Сs, P., _Some unconventional problems in number theory_. Acta Math. Acad. Sci.
Hungar. (1979).
-/

open Real

namespace Erdos693

/-- An integer $m$ has a divisor in the open interval $(n, 2n)$. -/
def hasDivisorIn (m n : вДХ) : Prop :=
  вИГ d, d вИ£ m вИІ n < d вИІ d < 2 * n

/--
Erd≈Сs Problem 693 [Er79e]:

Let $k \geq 2$ and $n$ be sufficiently large depending on $k$. Let
$A = \{a_1 < a_2 < \cdots\}$ be the set of integers in $[n, n^k]$ which have a divisor
in $(n, 2n)$. Is the maximum gap $\max_i (a_{i+1} - a_i) \leq (\log n)^{O(1)}$?
-/
@[category research open, AMS 11]
theorem erdos_693 :
    answer(sorry) вЖФ
    вИА k : вДХ, k вЙ• 2 вЖТ
    вИГ C : вДХ,
    вИГ NвВА : вДХ, вИА n : вДХ, n вЙ• NвВА вЖТ
    вИА a b : вДХ, n вЙ§ a вЖТ a < b вЖТ b вЙ§ n ^ k вЖТ
    hasDivisorIn a n вЖТ hasDivisorIn b n вЖТ
    (вИА m : вДХ, a < m вЖТ m < b вЖТ ¬ђhasDivisorIn m n) вЖТ
    (b : вДЭ) - (a : вДЭ) вЙ§ (Real.log (n : вДЭ)) ^ C := by
  sorry

end Erdos693

content.text:
Both issues fixed:
- Renamed `hasDivisorIn` вЖТ `HasDivisorIn` across all 5 occurrences (¬І5 predicate naming)
- Added a brief problem description to the module docstring (¬І3)
